title: Swarm Robotics for Bio-Logistics
coreConcept: The coordination of large numbers of relatively simple robots to perform complex logistical tasks in pharmaceutical and biological settings.
theory:
## Emergent Coordination

### Swarm Intelligence
Implementing bio-inspired algorithms (e.g., Ant Colony Optimization) to coordinate robots in a warehouse or lab. Robots use **Autonomous Navigation** to avoid collisions while optimizing for throughput.

### Decentralized Communication
Leveraging **Distributed Systems** principles to ensure the swarm functions even if individual robots or central controllers fail. Decisions are made locally based on neighbor states.

### Bio-Logistics Constraints
Managing the transport of temperature-sensitive biological samples (Cold Chain) where robots must prioritize speed and environmental monitoring to maintain sample viability.
keyTakeaways: Design decentralized control laws for robotic swarms.
Implement emergent behaviors for task allocation.
Optimize multi-agent path planning in constrained labs.
Manage cold-chain logistics using autonomous agents.
